policies.displayName: Enable client to auto update
policies.description:
This is an old policy for auto update. It is now recommended to use the new AU2 series policies.

Default setting for EXE installation is enabled, default setting for MSI installation is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will auto update itself.

If this setting is disabled, client will not auto update itself.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable creating a desktop shortcut
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, no shortcut will be created on desktop.

If this setting is disabled, a shortcut will be created on desktop the first time the user logs in.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Auto start client after reboot
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will automatically start and show itself in task bar after system reboot.

If this policy is set to false, client will not automatically start after system reboot.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Auto start client after reboot in system tray
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will automatically start and move itself to system tray without showing on task bar after system reboot.

If this policy is set to false, client will not automatically start after system reboot.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Always use WinINet to do proxy resolve
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is enabled, the client will always use WinINet to do proxy resolve.

If this policy is disabled, the client will use WinHTTP to do proxy resolve first, WinINet will be used as the failover resolver.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable wininet proxy resolver use curl to download PAC
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, wininet proxy resolver won't try to download pac by curl.

If this policy is disabled, wininet proxy resolver will try to download pac by curl.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enabling this option determines whether non-PK proxy requests should go directly based on the result of the PK request
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, and the PK request results in the proxy winning, it will not affect subsequent requests. However, if the PK request results in direct connection winning, all subsequent non-PK requests will use direct connections.

If this policy is disabled, client will have no effect.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set the delay time for proxy requests in PK requests
policies.description:
Default policy is 500.

This policy depends on TryPKRequestDirectFirst. If TryPKRequestDirectFirst is disabled, this policy will have no effect. It sets the delay for proxy requests in PK requests to increase the likelihood of direct connections winning, helps reduce the load on proxy servers.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set default SSO URL for client login
policies.description:
Default URL is empty, client will manually enter the SSO URL for SSO log in.

Allows you to set the SSO URL.

policies.displayName: Force login with SSO only
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will be forced to login with SSO only.

If this policy is set to false, client will not be forced to login with SSO.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

If another setting 'Disable login with SSO' is enabled, this policy will be voided and makes no impact no matter this policy is set to true or false.

policies.displayName: Disable login with Google OAuth
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with Google will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with Google will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable login with Facebook OAuth
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with Facebook will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with Facebook will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable login with SSO
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with SSO will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with SSO will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable login with Magic Code
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with Magic Code will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with Magic Code will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable login with Microsoft
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with Microsoft will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with Microsoft will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable login with email and password
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with email and password will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with email will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Set account IDs that client is restricted to join meeting hosted by
policies.description:
Default setting is empty, client can join meeting hosted by any account IDs.

Allow you to set multiple account IDs that client is restricted to join meeting hosted by. Multiple account IDs can by separated with ,.

For example: 111111,22222

policies.displayName: Client will minimize to system tray and not show on task bar when closed
policies.description:
Default policy is true.

If this policy is set to true, client will minimize to system tray and not show on task bar when closed.

If this policy is set to false, client will minimize to task bar when closed.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Set email domains that client is restricted to log in with
policies.description:
Default setting is empty, client can log in with any email domains.

Allow you to set multiple email domains that client is restricted to log in with. Multiple email accounts can by separated with &.

For example: abc.com&zoom.us

policies.displayName: Client will use personal meeting ID to start meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will use personal meeting ID to start meeting.

If this policy is set to false, client will use a random generated meeting ID to start meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Client will block connection to untrusted SSL certificates
policies.description:
Default policy is true.

If this policy is true, client will show a warning dialog when connecting to untrusted SSL certificates, and there is no Trust button to allow user to bypass and continue.

If this setting is false, client will show a warning dialog when connecting to untrusted SSL certificates, but there is a Trust button to allow user to bypass and continue.

If this policy is not set, client use will default policy.

policies.displayName: Enable to switch between Zoom commercial(default) cloud and FedRAMP cloud
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will be able to switch between Zoom commercial (default) cloud and FedRAMP cloud.

If this policy is set to false, client will work with Zoom commercial (default) cloud only.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Enable embed browser for SSO
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will use embedded  browser for SSO.

If this policy is set to false, client will launch system default browser for SSO.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Enable SSO login using WebView2 browser kernel
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

This policy depends on EnableEmbedBrowserForSSO, if EnableEmbedBrowserForSSO is false, EnableNewWebView2SSO will not work.

If this policy is set to true, the SSO login page will use Microsoft's latest WebView2 browser kernel.

If this policy is set to false, the SSO login page will use Microsoft's traditional IE browser kernel.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set and lock the default SSO URL for client login
policies.description:
Default URL is empty, client will manually enter the SSO URL for SSO log in.

If your organization is using FedRAMP cloud, you can input URL like: your-org-name.zoomgov.com; if your organization is using CN cluster, you can input URL like: your-org-name.zoom.com.

Allows you to set and lock the SSO URL.

policies.displayName: Client will keep signed in
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will always enable 'remember me' feature.

If this setting is false, user needs to input credential for every time of Zoom application launch.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting or user setting.

policies.displayName: Set default web domain for client login and join meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is a valid string, client will always use this domain to login and join meeting.

If this setting is empty, client will use the default cloud "https://zoom.us" and/or "https://zoom.com" to login and join meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enforce sign in to join Zoom meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is no sign in restriction to join Zoom meeting.

If this setting is true, Zoom app cannot join meeting before signing in.

If this setting is false, Zoom app can join meeting without signing in.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable login with phone
policies.description:
Default setting is false.

If this setting is false, login with Phone will be disabled.

If this setting is true, login with Phone will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, it will be disable if IT installed the client, otherwise use app-default (enable)

policies.displayName: Set to enable login via Apple OAuth
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with Apple will be enabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with Apple will be disabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to use portrait view
policies.description:
Default policy is not to use portrait view.

If this setting is true, client will use portrait view.

If this setting is false, client will not use portrait view.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to embed one specified user agent string for all HTTP requests from Zoom client application; this piece of string will be appended to the end of regular user agent string.
policies.description:
Default policy is to use regular user agent string only.

If this setting is not empty, client will append the value to the regular user agent string.

If this setting is empty, client will use regular user agent string.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to embed one specified device tag string for all HTTP requests from Zoom client application; this piece of string will be appended to the head of the regular HTTP requests.
policies.description:
Default policy is to ignore this tag.

If this setting is not empty, client will append the value to the HTTP request head.

If this setting is empty, client will ignore the specified head.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable to send dumps to Zoom backend to improve the service
policies.description:
Default policy is no automatically uploading dumps.

If this setting is true, client will automatically upload dumps.

If this setting is false, client will not automatically upload dumps.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable to send critical-failure-logs to Zoom backend to improve the service
policies.description:
Default policy is no automatically uploading critical-failure-logs.

If this setting is true, client will automatically upload critical-failure-logs.

If this setting is false, client will not automatically upload critical-failure-logs.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set screen reader alerts
policies.description:
If this policy is a valid string, client will parse the string and set the selected options on.

If this policy is empty, client will set all options on.

String represents a numeric value. The values and options enabled are as follows:
IM CHAT RECEIVED = 1
PARTICIPANT JOINED LEFTED MEETING = 2
PARTICIPANT JOINED LEFED WAITING ROOM = 4
AUDIO MUTED UNMUTED BY HOST = 8
VIDEO STOPED BY HOST = 16
SCREEN SHARING STARTED STOPED BY PARTICIPANT = 32
RECORDING PERMISSION GRANTED REVOKED = 64
PUB MEETING CHAT RECEIVED = 128
PRI MEETING CHAT RECEIVED = 256
MEETING FILE UPLOADED = 512
CC AVAILABLE = 1024
CC TYPING PRIVILEGE GRANTED REVOKED = 2048
HOST PRIVILEGE GRANTED REVOKED = 4096
COHOST PRIVILEGE GRANTED REVOKED = 8192
REMOTE CONTROL PERMISSION GRANTED REVOKED = 16384
LIVESTREAM STARTED STOPPED = 32768
PARTICIPANT RAISED LOWERED HAND = 65536
QA QUESTION RECEIVED = 131072
QA ANSWER RECEVIEVED = 262144
ROLE CHANGED TO PANELIST = 524288
ROLE CHANGED TO ATTENDEE = 1048576

policies.displayName: Set to integrate Zoom with Outlook
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will integrate Zoom with Outlook.

If this policy is set to false, client will not integrate Zoom with Outlook.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Disable to create a specified PK request with direct connection.
policies.description:
In common case, there always be a direct connection request for login/join meeting requests; if this option is ON, the client application will try with system proxy (if have) at first

Default policy is false.

If this setting is true, client will disable the direct connection PK request.

If this setting is false, client will always try direct connection even there is proxy server configuration.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable all direct connections to Zoom web service
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this setting is true, client will disable the direct connection to Zoom web service.

If this setting is false, client will always try direct connection even there is proxy server configuration.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set proxy server to client with PAC URL
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is a valid string, client will adopt the proxy setting as the PAC URL pointed.

If this setting is empty, client will use other proxy settings.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set proxy server to client as named proxy like server:port
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is a valid string, client will parse the string and use it as the proxy server.

If this setting is empty, client will use other proxy settings.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set proxy by-pass rule to client
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is a valid string, client will parse the string and use it as the proxy by-pass rule.

If this setting is empty, client will use other proxy settings.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set device policy's token from web settings
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this setting is a valid string, client will append the value to the web request and get policies from web settings.

If this setting is empty, client will not append the value.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable keep signed in if signing in via SSO
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, user needs to input SSO credential for every time of Zoom application launch.

If this policy is false, client will keep signed in if signing in via SSO.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable keep signed in if signing in via Google
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, user needs to input Google credential for every time of Zoom application launch.

If this policy is false, client will keep signed in if signing in via Google.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable keep signed in if signing in via Facebook
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, user needs to input Facebook credential for every time of Zoom application launch.

If this policy is false, client will keep signed in if signing in via Facebook.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable Zoom Apps feature
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will disable Zoom Apps feature.

If this policy is disabled, client will enable Zoom Apps feature.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable client to auto update silently
policies.description:
This is an old policy for auto update. It is now recommended to use the new AU2 series policies.

Default policy is enabled.

This policy depends on EnableClientAutoUpdate, if EnableClientAutoUpdate is disabled, EnableSilentAutoUpdate will not work, even if it is enabled. (For msi package, EnableClientAutoUpdate is disabled by default. For exe, it is enabled)

If this policy is enabled, client will auto update itself silently.

If this policy is disabled, client will auto update itself and prompt users the update dialog.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set a whitelist to ignore the revocation check error
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is empty, client will ignore this policy.

If this policy is a valid string, which means the whitelist domain has no protocol, path, params and is divided by ";", client will ignore the revocation error for the whitelist domain.

Example settings "foo.com;bar.com"

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set the proxy detect interval in seconds
policies.description:
Default setting value is 0.

If this policy is set, client will use to set the proxy detect interval and will enable PAC cache.

If this policy is 0, client will use the hardcode detect interval 30 min.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enforce app local sign in to join Zoom meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is no sign in restriction to join Zoom meeting.

If this policy is enabled, Zoom app cannot join meeting before signing in locally.

If this policy is disabled, Zoom app can join meeting without signing in locally.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable client to check the latest version instead of the stable version
policies.description:
This is an old policy for auto update. It is now recommended to use the new AU2 series policies.

Default policy is disabled.

This policy depends on EnableClientAutoUpdate, if EnableClientAutoUpdate is disabled, AlwaysCheckLatestVersion will not work, even if it is enabled. (For msi package, EnableClientAutoUpdate is disabled by default. For exe, it is enabled)

If EnableSilentAutoUpdate is enabled(default is enabled), it's better to use SetUpdatingChannel instead of this policy.

If this policy is enabled, client will check the latest version in the background.

If this policy is disabled, client will check the stable version in the background.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set enrollment token for cloud-based device management
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is empty, client will ignore this policy.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use this string to enroll to cloud-based device management.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable client to launch SSO login web page automatically
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will not launch SSO login web page automatically.

If this policy is disabled, client will launch SSO login web page automatically.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set the specified update channel when silent auto update is enabled
policies.description:
This is an old policy for auto update. It is now recommended to use the new AU2 series policies.

Default policy is 0, client will use 'Slow' as updating channel when the client auto updates silently.

This policy depends on EnableSilentAutoUpdate, if EnableSilentAutoUpdate is disabled, SetUpdatingChannel will not work.

If this policy is a valid integer, client will use the specified channel listed as below:
0 - Slow
1 - Fast

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable client update from 32 bit to 64 bit
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will not update from 32 bit to 64 bit.

If this policy is disabled, client will update from 32 bit to 64 bit.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable Zoom Apps for users who are not authenticated
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

This policy works independently of old flag 'DisableZoomApps'.

If this policy is enabled, client will disable Zoom Apps for users who are not authenticated.

If this policy is disabled, client will enable Zoom Apps for users who are not authenticated.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set up to use the different policy to do proxy pre-detection
policies.description:
Default policy is 0.

If this policy is 0, use the default policy to do pre-detection.

If this policy is 1, use wininet to do pre-detection(In some PAC cases, it will be faster).

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable double-checking winhttp proxy resolver query results
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, winhttp proxy resolver query will skip the step for double check result via wininet.

If this policy is disabled, it will have no effect.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to support Windows extend protection for NTLM authentication
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will support Windows extend protection for NTLM authentication.

If this policy is disabled, client will not support Windows extend protection for NTLM authentication.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable the ability to sign into multiple Zoom accounts and switch accounts
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will disable the ability to sign into multiple Zoom accounts and switch accounts.

If this policy is disabled, client will not disable the ability to sign into multiple Zoom accounts and switch accounts.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to hide the sign up button
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, the client will hide the sign up button on the login dialog.

If this policy is disabled, the client will not hide the sign up button on the login dialog.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to hide the onboarding process for new users
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, the client will hide the onboarding process for new users.

If this policy is disabled, the client will not hide the onboarding process for new users.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to hide 'Sign out' button
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, the client will hide the 'Sign out' button.

If this policy is disabled, the client will not hide the 'Sign Out' button.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to hide the 'Discover What's New' menu
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, the client will hide the 'Discover What's New' menu.

If this policy is disabled, the client will not hide the 'Discover What's New' menu.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable P2P Stun Traffic
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will disable p2p stun traffic.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not disable p2p stun traffic.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable app if ZDM enrollment fails
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will disable the Zoom app if enrollment of this device into Zoom Device Management fails.
If this policy is disabled, client will not disable the Zoom app if enrollment of this device into Zoom Device Management fails.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable saving meeting statistics data to local log file
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this setting is true, client will save meeting statistics data to local log file.

If this setting is false, client will not save meeting statistics data to local log file.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable reporting of problems on the client
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will not be able to report problems.
If this policy is disabled, the client can report problems.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable full dump generation on windows platform client
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will not be able to generate full dump.
If this policy is disabled, the client can generate full dump.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable download webview on windows platform client
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will not be able to download webview.
If this policy is disabled, the client can download webview.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable warning prompts for 32-bit clients
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the 32-bit client will prompt a warning.
If this policy is disabled, the 32-bit client do not prompt a warning.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable ProxyDetect logic for 30 mins refresh and networkchange
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the Windows client will trigger proxy detect logic for 30 mins and network change situation.
If this policy is disabled, the Windows client will not trigger proxy detect logic for 30 mins and network change situation.
If this policy is not set, the Windows client will not trigger proxy detect logic for 30 mins and network change situation.

policies.displayName: Disable the new setting UI
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will not use the new setting UI.
If this policy is disabled, the client will use the new setting UI.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable the new login UI
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will use the new login UI.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not use the new login UI.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Configure the webview proxy server
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.
If this policy is empty or invalid, client will ignore this policy.
If this policy is a valid string, client will use this string as the webview proxy server.
If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set preferences for client exit
policies.description:
This policy has a higher priority than CloseToSystemTray.

Default policy is 0.

If this policy is set to 0, the client will minimize to the system tray when the main window is closed.

If this policy is set to 1, the client will fully exit when the main window is closed.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable the screenshot feature in the desktop client
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, all screenshot functions on the client will be disabled.
If this policy is disabled, all screenshot functions on the client are working properly.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enabling the 'journal_mode=DELETE' option in SQLite
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will enable the 'journal_mode=DELETE' option in SQLite to enhance compatibility and performance in inefficient storage environments such as NFS or NAS.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not enable this option in SQLite.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Automatically detect voice activity to create my notes
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will notify user when voice activity is detected on specified apps.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not notify user when voice activity is detected on specified apps.
If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable the auto-update feature
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will auto update itself.

If this policy is disabled, client will not auto update itself.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set the specified update channel when auto-update feature is enabled
policies.description:
Default policy is empty, client will subscribe 'Slow' as update channel when the auto-update feature is enabled.

This policy depends on AU2_EnableAutoUpdate, if AU2_EnableAutoUpdate is disabled, AU2_SetUpdateChannel will not work.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use the specified channel listed as below:
'0' - Slow
'1' - Fast

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to show 'Zoom Updates' in the general settings
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will show 'Zoom Updates' in the general settings.

If this policy is disabled, client will hide 'Zoom Updates' in the general settings.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to show a banner on the homepage when updates are available
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will show a banner on the homepage when updates are available.

If this policy is disabled, client will not show a banner on the homepage when updates are available.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to prompt the user to update when exiting a meeting or webinar if an update is available
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will prompt the user to update when exiting a meeting or webinar if an update is available.

If this policy is disabled, client will not prompt the user to update when exiting a meeting or webinar if an update is available.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to show a toast indicating that an update has been successfully applied
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will show a toast indicating that an update has been successfully applied on the next launch after an update has been applied.

If this policy is disabled, client will not show a toast indicating that an update has been successfully applied on the next launch after an update has been applied.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable the manual 'Check for Updates' option
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will enable the 'Check for Updates' option.

If this policy is disabled, client will disable the 'Check for Updates' option.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Specify the update target version
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is empty or invalid, client will ignore this policy.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use this string as the update target version.

If the Zoom's version is lower than the specific version, the client will auto update to the specific version. If the Zoom's version is higher than the specific version, the client will not do anything.

The format of this string should be major.minor.patch.build, for example: "5.10.4.5035"

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set a local storage url for update
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

This policy depends on AU2_DeploySpecificVersion, if AU2_DeploySpecificVersion is empty, AU2_Update3rdStorageUrl will not work.

Stored installation package needs to be named according to the following rules: ZoomInstallerFull.msi, x64_ZoomInstallerFull.msi, winarm64_ZoomInstallerFull.msi.

If the user's installation package can be downloaded through such a link: "http://127.0.0.1/x64_ZoomInstallerFull.msi", this string should be "http://127.0.0.1".

If this policy is a valid string, client will use this string as the update download url when the target version of the update is specified.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set auto update schedule
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is empty or invalid, client will download the update package at anytime.

If this policy is a valid string, client will download the update package within the specified time period.

The format of this string should be HHMM-HHMM, for example: "0900-1800" means 09:00 to 18:00, "1800-0400" means 18:00 to the next day 04:00.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable auto update installation when idle
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

This policy depends on AU2_EnableAutoUpdate, if AU2_EnableAutoUpdate is disabled, AU2_InstallAtIdleTime will not work.

If this policy is enabled, client will enable auto update installation when idle.

If this policy is disabled, client will disable auto update installation when idle.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to install auto updates within a specified time period
policies.description:
Default policy is "0000-0400".

This policy depends on AU2_InstallAtIdleTime, if AU2_InstallAtIdleTime is disabled, AU2_SafeUpgradePeriod will not work.

If this policy is empty or invalid, client will install auto updates within the default time period when idle.

If this policy is a valid string, client will install auto updates within the specified time period when idle.

The format of this string should be HHMM-HHMM, for example: "0900-1800" means 09:00 to 18:00, "1800-0400" means 18:00 to the next day 04:00.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set the update channel list
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is empty or invalid, client will set Slow and Fast as the default update channel list.

If this policy is a valid string, client will set the update channel list according to the string.

The format of this string should be {channel id}:{channel name} and use semicolon to separate different channels, for example: "0:Slow" will add Slow to the update channel list, "0:Slow;1:Fast" will add Slow and Fast to the update channel list.

The following are the two public update channels:
"0" - Slow
"1" - Fast

Note:
1.Slow will be added to the list even if it is not written to string.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set manual check for updates to the version defined by Auto Update
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

This policy depends on AU2_EnableAutoUpdate and AU2_EnableManualUpdate, if AU2_EnableAutoUpdate or AU2_EnableManualUpdate is disabled, AU2_LimitManualUpdateVersion will not work.

If this policy is enabled, client will set manual check for updates to the version defined by Auto Update.

If this policy is disabled, client will set manual check for updates to the latest version.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will disable and hide link preview feature in chat
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable and hide link preview feature in chat.

If this setting is disabled, link preview feature will be enabled in chat.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to move messages with new replies to the bottom of the chat
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will move messages with new replies to the bottom of the chat.

If this setting is disabled, client will not move messages with new replies to the bottom of the chat.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to show audio message button
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will show audio message button.

If this setting is disabled, client will not show audio message button.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to show notification banner on screen util dismissed
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will show notification banner on screen util dismissed.

If this setting is disabled, client will not show notification banner on screen util dismissed.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to play sound when receive a new message
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will play sound when receive a new message.

If this setting is disabled, client will not play sound when receive a new message.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to show message preview
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will show message preview.

If this setting is disabled, client will not show message preview.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to mute chat notifications while in a meeting or internal call
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will mute chat notifications while in a meeting or internal call.

If this setting is disabled, client will not mute chat notifications while in a meeting or internal call.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to show video message button
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will show video message button.

If this setting is disabled, client will not show video message button.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to show profile photos next to direct messages
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will show profile photos next to direct messages.

If this setting is disabled, client will not show profile photos next to direct messages.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Keep only one section open in Team Chat
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client only allow one section opened at a time.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not limit sections
If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Organize chats by section
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client organize chats by section.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not Organize chats by section
If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable IM file transfer
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will disable IM file transfer feature when current domain is not exist in the list of AllowedIMFileTransferDomain.
If this policy is disabled, the client will use the default setting.
If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Allowed IM file transfer domain list
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.
If this policy is a valid string(for example:zoom1.us),and DisableIMFileTransfer is enabled, the client will only allow the domain zoom1.us to IM transfer feature. Multiple domains can be separated with & (for example: zoom1.us&zoom2.us)
If this setting is empty, and DisableIMFileTransfer is enabled, client will disable IM transfer feature for all domains.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Setting up message notifications preferences for Team Chat
policies.description:
This policy is exactly same as the manual setting: "Settings"-"Team Chat"-"Message Preferences". Its default policy is 1, client will default to notify direct messages, mentions, and replies.

If this policy is a valid integer, client will use the specified type listed as below:
0 - All messages
1 - Direct messages, mentions, and replies
2 - Off

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Keep all unread messages at the top of recent chats
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this setting is true, client will keep all unread messages at the top of recent chats.

If this setting is false, client will not keep all unread messages at the top of recent chats.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Show unread message count for channels
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this setting is true, client will show unread message count for channels.

If this setting is false, client will not show unread message count for channels.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Show unread message count for meeting chats
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this setting is true, client will show unread message count for meeting chats.

If this setting is false, client will not show unread message count for meeting chats.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Where to start when viewing unread messages in a channel
policies.description:
Its default policy is 0, client will default to start viewing unread messages at the first unread message.

If this policy is a valid integer, client will use the specified type listed as below:
0 - Start at the first unread message
1 - Start at the latest unread message

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Setting keywords as the basis for triggering message notifications
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.
If this policy is a valid string, the client will trigger message notifications based on these keywords.
If this setting is empty, client will trigger message notifications normally.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Use this language when translating messages
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.
If this policy is a valid string(for example:en), the client will use this language when translating messages
If this setting is empty, the client will use the default language when translating messages.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Turn off video camera when joining meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will turn off video camera when joining meeting.

If this policy is set to false, client will turn on video camera when joining meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will default policy.

policies.displayName: Auto connect audio with VoIP when joining meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will automatically connect audio with VoIP when joining meeting.

If this policy is set to false, client will not automatically connect audio with VoIP when joining meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Enable dual monitor if system supports
policies.description:
Default setting is disbled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enabled dual monitor mode for meeting.

If this setting is disabled, client will disable dual monitor mode for meeting.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will enter full screen mode when viewing shared screen
policies.description:
Default policy is true.

If this policy is set to true, client will enter full screen mode when viewing shared screen.

If this policy is set to false, client will stay in windowed mode when viewing shared screen.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Client will resize content to fit the window when viewing shared screen
policies.description:
Default policy is true.

If this policy is set to true, client will resize content to fit the window when viewing shared screen.

If this policy is set to false, client will not resize content to fit the window when viewing shared screen.

If this policy is not set, client will default policy.

policies.displayName: Client will capture and send camera video in HD 720p format
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will capture and send camera video in HD 720p format.

If this setting is disabled, client will capture and send camera video in 360p format.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: All applications can be controlled during remote control session
policies.description:
Default policy is true.

If this policy is set to true, all applications can be controlled during remote control session.

If this policy is set to false, system applications cannot be controlled during remote control session.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Set path for local meeting recording files
policies.description:
Default path is User/Document folder.

Allows you to set path for local meeting recording files.

For example: C:\MyZoomRecordings

policies.displayName: Disable Aero mode when screen sharing on Windows 7 system
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, Aero mode will be disabled when screen sharing on Windows 7 system.

If this setting is disabled, Aero mode will not be disabled when screen sharing on Windows 7 system.

If this setting is not set, client will default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will always show meeting controls
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will always show meeting controls.

If this policy is set to false, some meeting controls like the meeting toolbar will automatically hide after idle for a few seconds and show on mouse movement or keyboard stroke event.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Client will automatically enter full screen mode when joining a meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will automatically enter full screen mode when joining a meeting.

If this policy is set to false, client will stay in windowed mode when joining a meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Client will automatically start My notes when joining a meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will automatically start My notes when joining a meeting.

If this policy is set to false, client will not automatically start My notes when joining a meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Client will prompt confirmation when leaving a meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is true.

If this policy is set to true, client will will prompt confirmation when leaving a meeting.

If this policy is set to false, client will leave a meting without prompt confirmation.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Client will show meeting timers
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will show meeting timers.

If this policy is set to false, client will not show meeting timers.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Client will mute VoIP when joining a meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will mute VoIP when joining a meeting.

If this policy is set to false, client will unmute VoIP when joining a meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Client will hide users with video turned off in gallery View
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will hide users with video turned off in gallery View.

If this policy is set to false, client will show users with video turned off in gallery View.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Client will adjust audio settings automatically
policies.description:
Default policy is true.

If this policy is set to true, client will adjust audio settings automatically.

If this policy is set to false, client will not adjust audio settings automatically, user has to manually adjust audio settings in Settings/Audio window.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

policies.displayName: Client will disable and hide Share Screen feature
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable and hide Share Screen feature.

If this setting is disabled, Share Screen feature will be enabled.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will disable and hide in meeting chat feature
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable and hide in meeting chat feature.

If this setting is disabled, in meeting chat feature will be enabled.

If this policy is not set, client will use local configured setting.

policies.displayName: Client will disable and hide the in meeting computer file-transfer feature
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable and hide the in meeting computer file-transfer feature.

If this setting is disabled, the in meeting computer file-transfer feature will be enabled.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will hide and disable the in meeting file-transfer from third-party, like Box, Dropbox.
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will hide and disable the in meeting third-party file-transfer feature.

If this setting is disabled, the in meeting third-party file-transfer feature will be enabled.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will disable and hide local recording feature
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable and hide local recording feature.

If this setting is disabled, local recording feature will be enabled.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will disable and hide cloud recording feature
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable and hide cloud recording feature.

If this setting is disabled, cloud recording feature will be enabled.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will disable and hide ability to send video
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable and hide ability to send video.

If this setting is disabled, client will be able to send video.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will disable and hide ability to receive video
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable and hide ability to receive video.

If this setting is disabled, client will be able to receive video.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will disable and hide ability to annotate over shared screen
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable and hide ability to annotate over shared screen.

If this setting is disabled, client will enable ability to annotate over shared screen.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will enable mirror effect of your video camera
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enable mirror effect over your video camera.

If this setting is disabled, client will disable mirror effect of your video camera.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will enable HID audio device control
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enable HID audio device control.

If this setting is disabled, client will disable HID audio device control.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Always show video preview dialog when joining a video meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will always show video preview dialog when joining a video meeting.

If this setting is false, client will never show video preview dialog before joining any meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting or user setting.

policies.displayName: Client will disable computer audio
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable computer audio.

If this setting is disabled, client will use computer audio.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will enable side-by-side mode
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enable side-by-side mode.

If this setting is disabled, client will disable side-by-side mode.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: During screen sharing, the person who is sharing won't allow others to control the shared content.
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will disable remote control feature.

If this setting is false, client will enable remote control feature.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

Web also has the same option, which has a higher privilege than this.

policies.displayName: Disallow meeting host to provide 1:1 remote support to another participant. This option depends on breakout room settings.
policies.description:
Default policy is true.

If this policy is true, client will disallow meeting host to provide 1:1 remote support.

If this setting is false, client will enable remote support feature.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

Web also has the same option, which has a higher privilege than this.

policies.displayName: Set the meeting sidebar to display in dark mode.
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, the side panel in meeting will display dark mode.

If this setting is disabled, the side panel appearance will follow setting.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will keep preserve original sound.
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will keep preserve original sound.

If this setting is disabled, client will not keep preserve original sound and use echo.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable touch up my appearance
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will enable touching up my appearance.

If this setting is false, client will disable touching up my appearance.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting or user setting.

policies.displayName: Set the value of "Touch up my appearance"
policies.description:
Default policy is 25.

This policy depends on EnableFaceBeauty, if EnableFaceBeauty is disabled, SetFaceBeautyValue will not work.

If this policy is a valid integer within 0 to 100, client will use the integer as the the value of "Touch up my appearance".

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable virtual background for meetings
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will disable virtual background for meetings.

If this setting is false, client will enable virtual background for meetings.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting or user setting.

policies.displayName: Disable white board feature(Classic)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will disable white board for meetings.

If this setting is false, client will enable white board for meetings.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting or user setting.

policies.displayName: Enable share audio feature
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will enable share audio feature.

If this setting is false, client will disable share audio feature.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting or user setting.

policies.displayName: Enable share video feature
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will enable share video feature.

If this setting is false, client will disable share video feature.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting or user setting.

policies.displayName: Disable to share with Zoom Rooms
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will disable the share to Zoom Rooms feature.

If this setting is false, client will enable the share to Zoom Rooms feature.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting or user setting.

policies.displayName: Disable share desktop feature
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will disable the share desktop feature.

If this setting is false, client will enable the share desktop feature.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting or user setting.

policies.displayName: Disable audio media stream over proxy server
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will disable the audio media stream over proxy server.

If this setting is false, client will enable the audio media stream over proxy server.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable video media stream over proxy server
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will disable the video media stream over proxy server.

If this setting is false, client will enable the video media stream over proxy server.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable screen sharing media stream over proxy server
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will disable the screen sharing media stream over proxy server.

If this setting is false, client will enable the screen sharing media stream over proxy server.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to use the system default microphone for VoIP
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will use the system default microphone for VoIP.

If this setting is false, client will use the user setting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to use the system default speaker for VoIP
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will use the system default speaker for VoIP.

If this setting is false, client will use the user setting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to use the system default communication speaker for VoIP
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will use the system default communication speaker for VoIP.

If this setting is false, client will use the user setting.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to use the system default communication microphone for VoIP
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will use the system default communication microphone for VoIP.

If this setting is false, client will use the user setting.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to show Zoom windows during screen sharing
policies.description:
Default policy is hidding Zoom windows during screen sharing.

If this setting is true, client will show Zoom windows during screen sharing.

If this setting is false, client will use the user setting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Set bandwidth limit up value, like "800" unit Kbps; if it's lower than 800Kbps, the value will be 800Kbps
policies.description:
Default policy is no limitation.

If this policy is set, client will restrict media uplink traffic with the limitation.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Set bandwidth limit down value, like "800" unit Kbps; if it's lower than 800Kbps, the value will be 800Kbps
policies.description:
Default policy is no limitation.

If this policy is set, client will restrict media downlink traffic with the limitation.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to ignore bandwidth limits
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will ignore bandwidth limits which set by web or GPO/MSI.

If this setting is disabled, client will not ignore bandwidth limits which set by web or GPO/MSI.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable independent media data port
policies.description:
Default policy is no different port.

IIf this setting is true, client will use different ports for media data as below:

AUDIO: 8803
AS: 8802
VIDEO: 8801

If this setting is false, client will use default setting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable Hardware Acceleration for Screen Sharing under Share Screen > Advanced
policies.description:
Default policy is GPU acceleration enabled.

If this setting is true, client will disable the GPU acceleration.

If this setting is false, client will enable the GPU acceleration.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable Hardware Acceleration for Annotations under Share Screen > Advanced
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this setting is true, client will use hardware acceleration to optimize annotations on the shared screen.

If this setting is false, client will not use hardware acceleration to optimize annotations.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to show "Call Room System" button on home panel
policies.description:
Default policy is no "Call Room System" button on home panel.

If this setting is true, client will show the "Call Room System" on home panel and supports to start meeting with Room System directly.

If this setting is false, client will hide above button.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to mute audio and stop video after locking the machine
policies.description:
Default policy is to send audio and stop video after locking the machine.

If this setting is true, client will mute audio and stop video after locking the machine.

If this setting is false, client will not mute audio and stop video after locking the machine.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to append caller name for Room system
policies.description:
Default policy is not to append caller name for Room system.

If this setting is true, client will append caller name for Room system.

If this setting is false, client will not append caller name for Room system.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable de-noise
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enable de-noise.

If this setting is disabled, client will disable de-noise.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable hardware acceleration for video processing
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enable hardware acceleration for video processing.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable hardware acceleration for video processing.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable hardware acceleration for sending video
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enable hardware acceleration for sending video.

If this setting is disabled, client will disable hardware acceleration for sending video.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable hardware acceleration for receiving video
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enable hardware acceleration for receiving video.

If this setting is disabled, client will disable hardware acceleration for receiving video.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to use TCP connection for screen sharing
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will use TCP connection for screen sharing.

If this setting is disabled, client will use UDP connection for screen sharing.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable spotlight my video when I speak
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will spotlight my video when I speak.

If this setting is disabled, client will not spotlight my video when I speak.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable display up to 49 participants per screen in gallery view
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will display up to 49 participants per screen in gallery view.

If this setting is disabled, client will not display up to 49 participants per screen in gallery view.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable reminding me before upcoming meetings
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will remind me before upcoming meetings with the minutes in setting panel.

If this setting is disabled, client will not remind me before upcoming meetings.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set specified video rendering method
policies.description:
Default policy is 0, client will use 'Auto' as video rendering method.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use the specified rendering method listed as below:
0 - Auto
1 - Direct3D11 Flip Mode
2 - Direct3D11
3 - Direct3D9
4 - GDI

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Client will optimize screen sharing for video clip when user shares directly to a Zoom Room
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will optimize screen sharing for video clip when user shares directly to a Zoom Room.

If this setting is disabled, client will not optimize screen sharing for video clip when user shares directly to a Zoom Room.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set specified sharing option when user shares directly to a Zoom Room
policies.description:
Default policy is 1, client will use 'Automatically share desktop' option when user shares directly to a Zoom Room.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use the specified option listed as below:
0 - Show all sharing options
1 - Automatically share desktop

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set specified sharing option when user shares screen in a meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is 0, client will use 'Show all sharing options' option when user shares screen in a meeting.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use the specified option listed as below:
0 - Show all sharing options
1 - Automatically share desktop

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set the specified screen capture mode
policies.description:
Default policy is 0, client will use 'Auto' as screen capture mode.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use the specified mode listed as below:
0 - Auto
1 - Legacy operating systems
2 - Capture with window filtering
3 - Advanced share with window filtering
4 - Advanced share without window filtering
5 - Secure share with window filtering

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable virtual filters
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable virtual filters for meetings.

If this setting is disabled, client will enable virtual filters for meetings.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to silence system notifications when sharing desktop
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will silence system notifications when sharing desktop.

If this setting is disabled, client will not silence system notifications when sharing desktop.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable hardware acceleration for virtual background
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enable hardware acceleration for virtual background.

If this setting is disabled, client will disable hardware acceleration for virtual background.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set a level for suppressing background noise
policies.description:
Default policy is 0, client will use 'Auto' by default.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use the specified type listed as below:
0 - Auto
1 - Low
2 - Medium
3 - High

The High option requires the audio enhancement package, and if not present, the client will default to Auto.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable 'signal processing by windows audio device drivers' for windows
policies.description:
This policy is exactly same as the manual setting: "Settings"-"Audio"-"Signal processing by windows audio device drivers". Its default policy is 0, client will default to Auto.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use the specified type listed as below:
0 - Auto
1 - Off(Windows - Raw)
2 - On(Windows - Default)

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to hide incoming calls while in a Zoom meeting
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will hide incoming calls while in a Zoom meeting.

If this setting is disabled, client will show incoming calls while in a Zoom meeting.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable share clipboard when user is screen sharing by default
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enable share clipboard when user is screen sharing by default.

If this setting is disabled, client will disable share clipboard when user is screen sharing by default.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable Closed Captioning
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable closed captioning.

If this setting is disabled, client will not disable closed captioning.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable QnA in the webinar
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable QnA in the webinar.

If this setting is disabled, client will not disable QnA in the webinar.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable slide control during a presention
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable slide control during a presention.

If this setting is disabled, client will not disable slide control during a presention.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable manual captions
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable manual captions.

If this setting is disabled, client will not disable manual captions.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable meeting reactions
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable meeting reactions.

If this setting is disabled, client will not disable meeting reactions.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable webinar reactions
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable webinar reactions.

If this setting is disabled, client will not disable webinar reactions.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable non-verbal feedback
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable non-verbal feedback.

If this setting is disabled, client will not disable non-verbal feedback.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enforce app local sign in to join Zoom webinar
policies.description:
Default policy is no sign in restriction to join Zoom webinar.

If this policy is enabled, Zoom app cannot join webinar before signing in locally.

If this policy is disabled, Zoom app can join webinar without signing in locally.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enforce sign in to join Zoom webinar
policies.description:
Default policy is no sign in restriction to join Zoom webinar.

If this setting is true, Zoom app cannot join webinar before signing in.

If this setting is false, Zoom app can join webinar without signing in.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable Hardware Acceleration for Video Sharing under Share Screen > Advanced
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will use hardware acceleration to optimize video sharing.

If this setting is disabled, client will not use hardware acceleration to optimize video sharing.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to adjust for low light
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will adjust for low light in the user's own video.

If this setting is disabled, client will not adjust for low light in the user's own video.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
