policies.displayName: Set to adjust for low light automatically
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

This policy depends on EnableLightAdaption, if EnableLightAdaption is disabled, EnableAutoLightAdaption will not work.

If this setting is enabled, client will adjust for low light in the user's own video automatically.

If this setting is disabled, client will not adjust for low light in the user's own video automatically.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set the manual value of "Adjust for low light"
policies.description:
Default policy is 20.

This policy depends on EnableLightAdaption and EnableAutoLightAdaption, if EnableFaceBeauty is enabled and EnableAutoLightAdaption is disabled, SetLightAdaptionManualValue will take effect.

If this policy is a valid integer within 0 to 100, client will use the integer as the the value of "Adjust for low light".

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to reset virtual backgrounds to default after each meeting
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will reset virtual backgrounds to default after each meeting.

If this setting is disabled, client will not reset virtual backgrounds to default after each meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable the ability of the host to broadcast messages to breakout rooms
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable the ability of the host to broadcast messages to breakout rooms.

If this setting is disabled, client will not disable the ability of the host to broadcast messages to breakout rooms.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to allow users to join other cloud's meetings without login if the EnforceSignInToJoin flag is set
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will allow users to join other cloud's meetings(e.g. ZFG) without login even if the EnforceSignInToJoin flag is set.

If this setting is disabled, client will respect EnforceSignInToJoin's logic.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable all intercloud features when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable all intercloud features if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable all intercloud features.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable meeting chat when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable meeting chat if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable meeting chat.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable screen share when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable screen share if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable screen share.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable meeting reactions when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable meeting reactions if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable meeting reactions.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable computer file-transfer when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable computer file-transfer if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable computer file-transfer.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable annotation when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable annotation if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable annotation.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable whiteboard when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable whiteboard if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable whiteboard.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable local recording when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable local recording if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable local recording.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable closed captioning when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable closed captioning if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable closed captioning.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable remote control when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable remote control if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable remote control.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable meeting polls when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable meeting polls if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable meeting polls.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable computer audio when joining other cloud's meetings
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will disable computer audio if the EnforceSignInToJoin and OverrideEnforceSigninIntercloud flags are set.

If this setting is disabled, when joining other cloud's meetings, client will not disable computer audio.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable launching an external app or a URL for incoming calls
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enable launching an external app or a URL for incoming calls.

If this setting is disabled, client will disable launching an external app or a URL for incoming calls.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable in-meeting whiteboard(New)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will disable in-meeting whiteboard.

If this policy is disabled, client will enable in-meeting whiteboard.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to hide self view in the meeting
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will hide self view in the meeting.

If this policy is disabled, client will not hide self view in the meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable video rendering post processing
policies.description:
This policy is exactly same as the manual setting: "Settings"-"Video"-"Advanced"-"Video Rendering Post Processing". Its default policy is 0, client will default to Auto.

If this policy is a valid integer, client will use the specified type listed as below:
0 - Auto
1 - Enable
2 - Disable

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set video capturing method
policies.description:
This policy is exactly same as the manual setting: "Settings"-"Video"-"Advanced"-"Video Capturing Method". Its default policy is 0, client will default to Auto.

If this policy is a valid integer, client will use the specified type listed as below:
0 - Auto
1 - Direct Show
2 - Media Foundation

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to show notification when a new recording is available
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will show notification when a new cloud recording is available.

If this setting is disabled, client will not show notification when a new cloud recording is available.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to disable webinar resources
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable webinar resources.

If this setting is disabled, client will not disable webinar resources.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set the default dialer for click-to-dial calls
policies.description:
Default setting is 0.

If this policy is a valid integer, client will use the specified dialer listed as below:
0 - Zoom Phone
1 - Zoom Contact Center

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable the diagnostic tool and basic detection of some services
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enable the diagnostic tool and basic detection of some services.

If this setting is disabled, client will not enable the diagnostic tool and basic detection of some services.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Force to enable the native video feature of Zoom Contact Center
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will force enable the native video feature of Zoom Contact Center.

If this setting is disabled, client will not force enable the native video feature of Zoom Contact Center.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Setting up message notifications for Zoom Contact Center
policies.description:
This policy is exactly same as the manual setting: "Settings"-"Audio"-"Contact Center message notifications". Its default policy is 1, client will default to Always play notifications.

If this policy is a valid integer, client will use the specified type listed as below:
0 - No effect
1 - Always play notifications
2 - Mute during meetings and calls
3 - Always mute notifications

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Turns on windows system enhancements for the speaker and microphone in use
policies.description:
This policy is exactly the same as the manual setting: "Settings"-"Audio"-"Advanced"-"Windows system audio enhancements". Its default policy is 0.

0 - Auto: Default value, using default Windows system setting.
1 - On: Turn on the windows system audio enhancements setting.
2 - Off: Turn off the windows system audio enhancements.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set audio capture and playback API
policies.description:
This policy is exactly the same as the manual setting: "Settings"-"Audio"-"Advanced"-"Audio capture and playback API". Its default policy is 0.

0 - Auto: Default value, using synchronous Windows API for audio capture and playback.
1 - Windows Audio Session API: Uses synchronous Windows API for audio capture and playback.
2 - Windows Runtime API: Uses asynchronous Windows Runtime API for audio capture and playback.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to enable mesh feature
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, this policy will allow the users to use mesh feature.

If this policy is disabled, this policy will not allow the users to use mesh feature even if they have a mesh plan.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set IP address for Zoom Mesh local detection
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is a valid IP address, this policy will allow admins to specify a valid IP address to be a UDP multicast IP for Zoom apps to declare themselves and detect each other in their local sub-network (255.255.255.0). Admins can choose other IP addresses within the range of [224.0.0.1, 239.255.255.255].

If this policy is empty or invalid, this policy will set as 224.0.0.1 by default.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set network port for for Zoom Mesh nodes
policies.description:
Default policy is 0.

If this policy is a valid port, client will use this port as the UDP multicast port to communicate with other Zoom Mesh nodes on the network.

If this policy is invalid or 0, this policy will set 36699 as the default port.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set network port range for Zoom Mesh parent-child node communication
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is a valid port range address, the client will use this range to set up listen sockets for connection requests between Mesh parents and children nodes. The nodes will also use this range to send signals and media data.

If this policy is invalid or empty, the client will use "18801-19800" as the default port range.

The format of this string should be "port-port", for example: "18801-19800".

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Allows users to join an external Webinar as a guest through another account's local Zoom Mesh-enabled network
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, this policy will allow users (not on your account) to join an external Webinar through the local Zoom Mesh-enabled network. Other requirements must be met, such as the device having the minimum supported client version for Mesh connections and the associated account setting in the web portal also enabled.

If this setting is disabled, this policy will not allow external users (not on your account) to join an external Webinar through the local Zoom Mesh-enabled network.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to hide 'Call Queues'
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, this policy will hide the 'Call Queues' in settings.

If this policy is disabled, this policy will not hide the 'Call Queues' in settings.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Set to hide 'Block Number' feature
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, this policy will hide the 'Block Number' feature.

If this policy is disabled, this policy will not hide the 'Block Number' feature.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable Zoom Notes for Meetings
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, this policy will disable Zoom Notes for Meetings.

If this policy is disabled, this policy will not disable Zoom Notes for Meetings.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable Meeting Summary with AI Companion
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, this policy will disable meeting summary with AI companion.

If this policy is disabled, this policy will not disable meeting summary with AI companion.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable AI Companion Questions
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, this policy will disable AI Companion questions.

If this policy is disabled, this policy will not disable AI Companion questions.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable Automatically copy invite link once the meeting starts
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.
If this setting is enabled, client will automatically copy invite link once the meeting starts.
If this setting is disabled, client will not automatically copy invite link once the meeting starts.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable Allow host to type or assign a participant to type
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.
If this setting is enabled, client will disable allow host to type or assign a participant to type.
If this setting is disabled,  client will not disable allow host to type or assign a participant to type.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable Zoom Docs for Meetings
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, this policy will disable Zoom Docs for Meetings.

If this policy is disabled, this policy will not disable Zoom Docs for Meetings.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Allow the client to switch to survivable mode
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.
If this policy is a valid survivability info string, the client can switch to survivable mode.
If this policy is empty, the client does not support survivable mode.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.

policies.displayName: Force Zoom Meetings on top of other apps during meetings
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, Zoom Meetings will be forced to sit on top of other apps during meetings.

If this policy is disabled, Zoom Meetings will not be forced to sit on top of other apps during meetings.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Disable local ASR feature
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, the client will not use local ASR feature.

If this policy is disabled, the client will use local ASR feature.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable the use of Office API to control PowerPoint slides
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, clients will be able to use the Office API to control PowerPoint slides.

If this policy is disabled, clients will be unable to use the Office API to control PowerPoint slides.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Prevents the Mesh feature from initiating any User Account Control (UAC) prompts
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, the client will not trigger any User Account Control (UAC) elevation for adding firewall rules required for the mesh feature.

If this policy is disabled, the client may trigger a User Account Control (UAC) prompt.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.

policies.displayName: Enable auto framing feature
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will enable auto-framing.

If this setting is false, client will disable auto-framing.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting or user setting.

policies.displayName: Enable client to auto update (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
This is an old policy for auto update. It is now recommended to use the new AU2 series policies.

Default setting for EXE installation is enabled, default setting for MSI installation is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will auto update itself.

If this setting is disabled, client will not auto update itself.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable creating a desktop shortcut (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, no shortcut will be created on desktop.

If this setting is disabled, a shortcut will be created on desktop the first time the user logs in.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Auto start client after reboot (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will automatically start and show itself in task bar after system reboot.

If this policy is set to false, client will not automatically start after system reboot.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Auto start client after reboot in system tray (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will automatically start and move itself to system tray without showing on task bar after system reboot.

If this policy is set to false, client will not automatically start after system reboot.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Always use WinINet to do proxy resolve (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is enabled, the client will always use WinINet to do proxy resolve.

If this policy is disabled, the client will use WinHTTP to do proxy resolve first, WinINet will be used as the failover resolver.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable wininet proxy resolver use curl to download PAC (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, wininet proxy resolver won't try to download pac by curl.

If this policy is disabled, wininet proxy resolver will try to download pac by curl.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enabling this option determines whether non-PK proxy requests should go directly based on the result of the PK request (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, and the PK request results in the proxy winning, it will not affect subsequent requests. However, if the PK request results in direct connection winning, all subsequent non-PK requests will use direct connections.

If this policy is disabled, client will have no effect.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set the delay time for proxy requests in PK requests (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is 500.

This policy depends on TryPKRequestDirectFirst. If TryPKRequestDirectFirst is disabled, this policy will have no effect. It sets the delay for proxy requests in PK requests to increase the likelihood of direct connections winning, helps reduce the load on proxy servers.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set default SSO URL for client login (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default URL is empty, client will manually enter the SSO URL for SSO log in.

Allows you to set the SSO URL.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Force login with SSO only (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will be forced to login with SSO only.

If this policy is set to false, client will not be forced to login with SSO.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.

If another setting 'Disable login with SSO' is enabled, this policy will be voided and makes no impact no matter this policy is set to true or false.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable login with Google OAuth (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with Google will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with Google will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable login with Facebook OAuth (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with Facebook will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with Facebook will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable login with SSO (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with SSO will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with SSO will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable login with Magic Code (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with Magic Code will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with Magic Code will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable login with Microsoft (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with Microsoft will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with Microsoft will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable login with email and password (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with email and password will be disabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with email will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set account IDs that client is restricted to join meeting hosted by (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is empty, client can join meeting hosted by any account IDs.

Allow you to set multiple account IDs that client is restricted to join meeting hosted by. Multiple account IDs can by separated with ,.

For example: 111111,22222
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Client will minimize to system tray and not show on task bar when closed (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is true.

If this policy is set to true, client will minimize to system tray and not show on task bar when closed.

If this policy is set to false, client will minimize to task bar when closed.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set email domains that client is restricted to log in with (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is empty, client can log in with any email domains.

Allow you to set multiple email domains that client is restricted to log in with. Multiple email accounts can by separated with &.

For example: abc.com&zoom.us
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Client will use personal meeting ID to start meeting (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will use personal meeting ID to start meeting.

If this policy is set to false, client will use a random generated meeting ID to start meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Client will block connection to untrusted SSL certificates (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is true.

If this policy is true, client will show a warning dialog when connecting to untrusted SSL certificates, and there is no Trust button to allow user to bypass and continue.

If this setting is false, client will show a warning dialog when connecting to untrusted SSL certificates, but there is a Trust button to allow user to bypass and continue.

If this policy is not set, client use will default policy.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enable to switch between Zoom commercial(default) cloud and FedRAMP cloud (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will be able to switch between Zoom commercial (default) cloud and FedRAMP cloud.

If this policy is set to false, client will work with Zoom commercial (default) cloud only.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enable embed browser for SSO (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will use embedded  browser for SSO.

If this policy is set to false, client will launch system default browser for SSO.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enable SSO login using WebView2 browser kernel (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

This policy depends on EnableEmbedBrowserForSSO, if EnableEmbedBrowserForSSO is false, EnableNewWebView2SSO will not work.

If this policy is set to true, the SSO login page will use Microsoft's latest WebView2 browser kernel.

If this policy is set to false, the SSO login page will use Microsoft's traditional IE browser kernel.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set and lock the default SSO URL for client login (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default URL is empty, client will manually enter the SSO URL for SSO log in.

If your organization is using FedRAMP cloud, you can input URL like: your-org-name.zoomgov.com; if your organization is using CN cluster, you can input URL like: your-org-name.zoom.com.

Allows you to set and lock the SSO URL.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Client will keep signed in (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, client will always enable 'remember me' feature.

If this setting is false, user needs to input credential for every time of Zoom application launch.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting or user setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set default web domain for client login and join meeting (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is a valid string, client will always use this domain to login and join meeting.

If this setting is empty, client will use the default cloud "https://zoom.us" and/or "https://zoom.com" to login and join meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to enforce sign in to join Zoom meeting (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is no sign in restriction to join Zoom meeting.

If this setting is true, Zoom app cannot join meeting before signing in.

If this setting is false, Zoom app can join meeting without signing in.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to enable login with phone (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is false.

If this setting is false, login with Phone will be disabled.

If this setting is true, login with Phone will be enabled.

If this setting is not set, it will be disable if IT installed the client, otherwise use app-default (enable)
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to enable login via Apple OAuth (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, login with Apple will be enabled.

If this setting is disabled, login with Apple will be disabled.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to use portrait view (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is not to use portrait view.

If this setting is true, client will use portrait view.

If this setting is false, client will not use portrait view.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to embed one specified user agent string for all HTTP requests from Zoom client application; this piece of string will be appended to the end of regular user agent string. (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is to use regular user agent string only.

If this setting is not empty, client will append the value to the regular user agent string.

If this setting is empty, client will use regular user agent string.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to embed one specified device tag string for all HTTP requests from Zoom client application; this piece of string will be appended to the head of the regular HTTP requests. (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is to ignore this tag.

If this setting is not empty, client will append the value to the HTTP request head.

If this setting is empty, client will ignore the specified head.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enable to send dumps to Zoom backend to improve the service (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is no automatically uploading dumps.

If this setting is true, client will automatically upload dumps.

If this setting is false, client will not automatically upload dumps.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enable to send critical-failure-logs to Zoom backend to improve the service (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is no automatically uploading critical-failure-logs.

If this setting is true, client will automatically upload critical-failure-logs.

If this setting is false, client will not automatically upload critical-failure-logs.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set screen reader alerts (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
If this policy is a valid string, client will parse the string and set the selected options on.

If this policy is empty, client will set all options on.

String represents a numeric value. The values and options enabled are as follows:
IM CHAT RECEIVED = 1
PARTICIPANT JOINED LEFTED MEETING = 2
PARTICIPANT JOINED LEFED WAITING ROOM = 4
AUDIO MUTED UNMUTED BY HOST = 8
VIDEO STOPED BY HOST = 16
SCREEN SHARING STARTED STOPED BY PARTICIPANT = 32
RECORDING PERMISSION GRANTED REVOKED = 64
PUB MEETING CHAT RECEIVED = 128
PRI MEETING CHAT RECEIVED = 256
MEETING FILE UPLOADED = 512
CC AVAILABLE = 1024
CC TYPING PRIVILEGE GRANTED REVOKED = 2048
HOST PRIVILEGE GRANTED REVOKED = 4096
COHOST PRIVILEGE GRANTED REVOKED = 8192
REMOTE CONTROL PERMISSION GRANTED REVOKED = 16384
LIVESTREAM STARTED STOPPED = 32768
PARTICIPANT RAISED LOWERED HAND = 65536
QA QUESTION RECEIVED = 131072
QA ANSWER RECEVIEVED = 262144
ROLE CHANGED TO PANELIST = 524288
ROLE CHANGED TO ATTENDEE = 1048576
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to integrate Zoom with Outlook (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will integrate Zoom with Outlook.

If this policy is set to false, client will not integrate Zoom with Outlook.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable to create a specified PK request with direct connection. (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
In common case, there always be a direct connection request for login/join meeting requests; if this option is ON, the client application will try with system proxy (if have) at first

Default policy is false.

If this setting is true, client will disable the direct connection PK request.

If this setting is false, client will always try direct connection even there is proxy server configuration.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable all direct connections to Zoom web service (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this setting is true, client will disable the direct connection to Zoom web service.

If this setting is false, client will always try direct connection even there is proxy server configuration.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set proxy server to client with PAC URL (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is a valid string, client will adopt the proxy setting as the PAC URL pointed.

If this setting is empty, client will use other proxy settings.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set proxy server to client as named proxy like server:port (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is a valid string, client will parse the string and use it as the proxy server.

If this setting is empty, client will use other proxy settings.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set proxy by-pass rule to client (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is a valid string, client will parse the string and use it as the proxy by-pass rule.

If this setting is empty, client will use other proxy settings.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set device policy's token from web settings (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this setting is a valid string, client will append the value to the web request and get policies from web settings.

If this setting is empty, client will not append the value.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable keep signed in if signing in via SSO (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, user needs to input SSO credential for every time of Zoom application launch.

If this policy is false, client will keep signed in if signing in via SSO.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable keep signed in if signing in via Google (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, user needs to input Google credential for every time of Zoom application launch.

If this policy is false, client will keep signed in if signing in via Google.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable keep signed in if signing in via Facebook (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is true, user needs to input Facebook credential for every time of Zoom application launch.

If this policy is false, client will keep signed in if signing in via Facebook.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to disable Zoom Apps feature (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will disable Zoom Apps feature.

If this policy is disabled, client will enable Zoom Apps feature.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enable client to auto update silently (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
This is an old policy for auto update. It is now recommended to use the new AU2 series policies.

Default policy is enabled.

This policy depends on EnableClientAutoUpdate, if EnableClientAutoUpdate is disabled, EnableSilentAutoUpdate will not work, even if it is enabled. (For msi package, EnableClientAutoUpdate is disabled by default. For exe, it is enabled)

If this policy is enabled, client will auto update itself silently.

If this policy is disabled, client will auto update itself and prompt users the update dialog.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set a whitelist to ignore the revocation check error (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is empty, client will ignore this policy.

If this policy is a valid string, which means the whitelist domain has no protocol, path, params and is divided by ";", client will ignore the revocation error for the whitelist domain.

Example settings "foo.com;bar.com"

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set the proxy detect interval in seconds (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting value is 0.

If this policy is set, client will use to set the proxy detect interval and will enable PAC cache.

If this policy is 0, client will use the hardcode detect interval 30 min.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to enforce app local sign in to join Zoom meeting (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is no sign in restriction to join Zoom meeting.

If this policy is enabled, Zoom app cannot join meeting before signing in locally.

If this policy is disabled, Zoom app can join meeting without signing in locally.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enable client to check the latest version instead of the stable version (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
This is an old policy for auto update. It is now recommended to use the new AU2 series policies.

Default policy is disabled.

This policy depends on EnableClientAutoUpdate, if EnableClientAutoUpdate is disabled, AlwaysCheckLatestVersion will not work, even if it is enabled. (For msi package, EnableClientAutoUpdate is disabled by default. For exe, it is enabled)

If EnableSilentAutoUpdate is enabled(default is enabled), it's better to use SetUpdatingChannel instead of this policy.

If this policy is enabled, client will check the latest version in the background.

If this policy is disabled, client will check the stable version in the background.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set enrollment token for cloud-based device management (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is empty, client will ignore this policy.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use this string to enroll to cloud-based device management.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable client to launch SSO login web page automatically (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will not launch SSO login web page automatically.

If this policy is disabled, client will launch SSO login web page automatically.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set the specified update channel when silent auto update is enabled (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
This is an old policy for auto update. It is now recommended to use the new AU2 series policies.

Default policy is 0, client will use 'Slow' as updating channel when the client auto updates silently.

This policy depends on EnableSilentAutoUpdate, if EnableSilentAutoUpdate is disabled, SetUpdatingChannel will not work.

If this policy is a valid integer, client will use the specified channel listed as below:
0 - Slow
1 - Fast

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable client update from 32 bit to 64 bit (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will not update from 32 bit to 64 bit.

If this policy is disabled, client will update from 32 bit to 64 bit.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to disable Zoom Apps for users who are not authenticated (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

This policy works independently of old flag 'DisableZoomApps'.

If this policy is enabled, client will disable Zoom Apps for users who are not authenticated.

If this policy is disabled, client will enable Zoom Apps for users who are not authenticated.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set up to use the different policy to do proxy pre-detection (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is 0.

If this policy is 0, use the default policy to do pre-detection.

If this policy is 1, use wininet to do pre-detection(In some PAC cases, it will be faster).

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable double-checking winhttp proxy resolver query results (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, winhttp proxy resolver query will skip the step for double check result via wininet.

If this policy is disabled, it will have no effect.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to support Windows extend protection for NTLM authentication (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will support Windows extend protection for NTLM authentication.

If this policy is disabled, client will not support Windows extend protection for NTLM authentication.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to disable the ability to sign into multiple Zoom accounts and switch accounts (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will disable the ability to sign into multiple Zoom accounts and switch accounts.

If this policy is disabled, client will not disable the ability to sign into multiple Zoom accounts and switch accounts.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to hide the sign up button (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, the client will hide the sign up button on the login dialog.

If this policy is disabled, the client will not hide the sign up button on the login dialog.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to hide the onboarding process for new users (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, the client will hide the onboarding process for new users.

If this policy is disabled, the client will not hide the onboarding process for new users.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to hide 'Sign out' butto (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, the client will hide the 'Sign out' button.

If this policy is disabled, the client will not hide the 'Sign Out' button.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to hide the 'Discover What's New' menu (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, the client will hide the 'Discover What's New' menu.

If this policy is disabled, the client will not hide the 'Discover What's New' menu.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable P2P Stun Traffic (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will disable p2p stun traffic.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not disable p2p stun traffic.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable app if ZDM enrollment fails (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will disable the Zoom app if enrollment of this device into Zoom Device Management fails.
If this policy is disabled, client will not disable the Zoom app if enrollment of this device into Zoom Device Management fails.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enable saving meeting statistics data to local log file (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

If this setting is true, client will save meeting statistics data to local log file.

If this setting is false, client will not save meeting statistics data to local log file.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable reporting of problems on the client (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will not be able to report problems.
If this policy is disabled, the client can report problems.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable full dump generation on windows platform client (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will not be able to generate full dump.
If this policy is disabled, the client can generate full dump.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable download webview on windows platform client (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will not be able to download webview.
If this policy is disabled, the client can download webview.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable warning prompts for 32-bit clients (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the 32-bit client will prompt a warning.
If this policy is disabled, the 32-bit client do not prompt a warning.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enable ProxyDetect logic for 30 mins refresh and networkchange (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the Windows client will trigger proxy detect logic for 30 mins and network change situation.
If this policy is disabled, the Windows client will not trigger proxy detect logic for 30 mins and network change situation.
If this policy is not set, the Windows client will not trigger proxy detect logic for 30 mins and network change situation.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable the new setting UI (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will not use the new setting UI.
If this policy is disabled, the client will use the new setting UI.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enable the new login UI (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will use the new login UI.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not use the new login UI.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Configure the webview proxy server (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.
If this policy is empty or invalid, client will ignore this policy.
If this policy is a valid string, client will use this string as the webview proxy server.
If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set preferences for client exit (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
This policy has a higher priority than CloseToSystemTray.

Default policy is 0.

If this policy is set to 0, the client will minimize to the system tray when the main window is closed.

If this policy is set to 1, the client will fully exit when the main window is closed.

If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable the screenshot feature in the desktop client (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, all screenshot functions on the client will be disabled.
If this policy is disabled, all screenshot functions on the client are working properly.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enabling the 'journal_mode=DELETE' option in SQLite (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will enable the 'journal_mode=DELETE' option in SQLite to enhance compatibility and performance in inefficient storage environments such as NFS or NAS.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not enable this option in SQLite.
If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Automatically detect voice activity to create my notes (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will notify user when voice activity is detected on specified apps.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not notify user when voice activity is detected on specified apps.
If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to enable the auto-update feature (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will auto update itself.

If this policy is disabled, client will not auto update itself.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set the specified update channel when auto-update feature is enabled (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty, client will subscribe 'Slow' as update channel when the auto-update feature is enabled.

This policy depends on AU2_EnableAutoUpdate, if AU2_EnableAutoUpdate is disabled, AU2_SetUpdateChannel will not work.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use the specified channel listed as below:
'0' - Slow
'1' - Fast

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to show 'Zoom Updates' in the general settings (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will show 'Zoom Updates' in the general settings.

If this policy is disabled, client will hide 'Zoom Updates' in the general settings.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to show a banner on the homepage when updates are available (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will show a banner on the homepage when updates are available.

If this policy is disabled, client will not show a banner on the homepage when updates are available.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to prompt the user to update when exiting a meeting or webinar if an update is available (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will prompt the user to update when exiting a meeting or webinar if an update is available.

If this policy is disabled, client will not prompt the user to update when exiting a meeting or webinar if an update is available.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to show a toast indicating that an update has been successfully applied (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will show a toast indicating that an update has been successfully applied on the next launch after an update has been applied.

If this policy is disabled, client will not show a toast indicating that an update has been successfully applied on the next launch after an update has been applied.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to enable the manual 'Check for Updates' option (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this policy is enabled, client will enable the 'Check for Updates' option.

If this policy is disabled, client will disable the 'Check for Updates' option.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Specify the update target version (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is empty or invalid, client will ignore this policy.

If this policy is a valid string, client will use this string as the update target version.

If the Zoom's version is lower than the specific version, the client will auto update to the specific version. If the Zoom's version is higher than the specific version, the client will not do anything.

The format of this string should be major.minor.patch.build, for example: "5.10.4.5035"

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set a local storage url for update (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

This policy depends on AU2_DeploySpecificVersion, if AU2_DeploySpecificVersion is empty, AU2_Update3rdStorageUrl will not work.

Stored installation package needs to be named according to the following rules: ZoomInstallerFull.msi, x64_ZoomInstallerFull.msi, winarm64_ZoomInstallerFull.msi.

If the user's installation package can be downloaded through such a link: "http://127.0.0.1/x64_ZoomInstallerFull.msi", this string should be "http://127.0.0.1".

If this policy is a valid string, client will use this string as the update download url when the target version of the update is specified.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set auto update schedule (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is empty or invalid, client will download the update package at anytime.

If this policy is a valid string, client will download the update package within the specified time period.

The format of this string should be HHMM-HHMM, for example: "0900-1800" means 09:00 to 18:00, "1800-0400" means 18:00 to the next day 04:00.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to enable auto update installation when idle (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

This policy depends on AU2_EnableAutoUpdate, if AU2_EnableAutoUpdate is disabled, AU2_InstallAtIdleTime will not work.

If this policy is enabled, client will enable auto update installation when idle.

If this policy is disabled, client will disable auto update installation when idle.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to install auto updates within a specified time period (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is "0000-0400".

This policy depends on AU2_InstallAtIdleTime, if AU2_InstallAtIdleTime is disabled, AU2_SafeUpgradePeriod will not work.

If this policy is empty or invalid, client will install auto updates within the default time period when idle.

If this policy is a valid string, client will install auto updates within the specified time period when idle.

The format of this string should be HHMM-HHMM, for example: "0900-1800" means 09:00 to 18:00, "1800-0400" means 18:00 to the next day 04:00.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set the update channel list (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.

If this policy is empty or invalid, client will set Slow and Fast as the default update channel list.

If this policy is a valid string, client will set the update channel list according to the string.

The format of this string should be {channel id}:{channel name} and use semicolon to separate different channels, for example: "0:Slow" will add Slow to the update channel list, "0:Slow;1:Fast" will add Slow and Fast to the update channel list.

The following are the two public update channels:
"0" - Slow
"1" - Fast

Note:
1.Slow will be added to the list even if it is not written to string.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set manual check for updates to the version defined by Auto Update (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.

This policy depends on AU2_EnableAutoUpdate and AU2_EnableManualUpdate, if AU2_EnableAutoUpdate or AU2_EnableManualUpdate is disabled, AU2_LimitManualUpdateVersion will not work.

If this policy is enabled, client will set manual check for updates to the version defined by Auto Update.

If this policy is disabled, client will set manual check for updates to the latest version.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Client will disable and hide link preview feature in chat (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will disable and hide link preview feature in chat.

If this setting is disabled, link preview feature will be enabled in chat.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to move messages with new replies to the bottom of the chat (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will move messages with new replies to the bottom of the chat.

If this setting is disabled, client will not move messages with new replies to the bottom of the chat.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to show audio message button (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will show audio message button.

If this setting is disabled, client will not show audio message button.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to show notification banner on screen util dismissed (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is disabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will show notification banner on screen util dismissed.

If this setting is disabled, client will not show notification banner on screen util dismissed.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to play sound when receive a new message (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will play sound when receive a new message.

If this setting is disabled, client will not play sound when receive a new message.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to show message preview (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will show message preview.

If this setting is disabled, client will not show message preview.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to mute chat notifications while in a meeting or internal call (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will mute chat notifications while in a meeting or internal call.

If this setting is disabled, client will not mute chat notifications while in a meeting or internal call.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to show video message button (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will show video message button.

If this setting is disabled, client will not show video message button.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Set to show profile photos next to direct messages (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is enabled.

If this setting is enabled, client will show profile photos next to direct messages.

If this setting is disabled, client will not show profile photos next to direct messages.

If this setting is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Keep only one section open in Team Chat (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client only allow one section opened at a time.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not limit sections
If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Organize chats by section (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client organize chats by section.
If this policy is disabled, the client will not Organize chats by section
If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Disable IM file transfer (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this policy is enabled, the client will disable IM file transfer feature when current domain is not exist in the list of AllowedIMFileTransferDomain.
If this policy is disabled, the client will use the default setting.
If this policy is not set, the client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Allowed IM file transfer domain list (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.
If this policy is a valid string(for example:zoom1.us),and DisableIMFileTransfer is enabled, the client will only allow the domain zoom1.us to IM transfer feature. Multiple domains can be separated with & (for example: zoom1.us&zoom2.us)
If this setting is empty, and DisableIMFileTransfer is enabled, client will disable IM transfer feature for all domains.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Setting up message notifications preferences for Team Chat (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
This policy is exactly same as the manual setting: "Settings"-"Team Chat"-"Message Preferences". Its default policy is 1, client will default to notify direct messages, mentions, and replies.

If this policy is a valid integer, client will use the specified type listed as below:
0 - All messages
1 - Direct messages, mentions, and replies
2 - Off

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Keep all unread messages at the top of recent chats (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this setting is true, client will keep all unread messages at the top of recent chats.

If this setting is false, client will not keep all unread messages at the top of recent chats.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Show unread message count for channels (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this setting is true, client will show unread message count for channels.

If this setting is false, client will not show unread message count for channels.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Show unread message count for meeting chats (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is disabled.
If this setting is true, client will show unread message count for meeting chats.

If this setting is false, client will not show unread message count for meeting chats.

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Where to start when viewing unread messages in a channel (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Its default policy is 0, client will default to start viewing unread messages at the first unread message.

If this policy is a valid integer, client will use the specified type listed as below:
0 - Start at the first unread message
1 - Start at the latest unread message

If this policy is not set, client will use the default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Setting keywords as the basis for triggering message notifications (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.
If this policy is a valid string, the client will trigger message notifications based on these keywords.
If this setting is empty, client will trigger message notifications normally.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Use this language when translating messages (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is empty.
If this policy is a valid string(for example:en), the client will use this language when translating messages
If this setting is empty, the client will use the default language when translating messages.

If this policy is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Turn off video camera when joining meeting (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will turn off video camera when joining meeting.

If this policy is set to false, client will turn on video camera when joining meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will default policy.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Auto connect audio with VoIP when joining meeting (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default policy is false.

If this policy is set to true, client will automatically connect audio with VoIP when joining meeting.

If this policy is set to false, client will not automatically connect audio with VoIP when joining meeting.

If this policy is not set, client will use default policy.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)

policies.displayName: Enable dual monitor if system supports (Recommendation Setting)
policies.description:
Default setting is disbled.

If this setting is enabled, client will enabled dual monitor mode for meeting.

If this setting is disabled, client will disable dual monitor mode for meeting.

If this setting is not set, client will use default setting.
categoryPath: Zoom Meetings - Default Settings (users can override)
